title: Bug: tasks with null priority crash the /tasks/sorted endpoint
description: Calling GET /api/tasks/sorted throws a NullPointerException when any task has a null priority field. This happens because the sort comparator calls getPriority().ordinal() without a null check.
stacktrace:
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "com.smartdev.hub.entity.Task$Priority.ordinal()" because the return value of "com.smartdev.hub.entity.Task.getPriority()" is null
	at com.smartdev.hub.service.TaskService.lambda$getTasksSortedByPriority$0(TaskService.java:52)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.SortedOps$SizedRefSortingSink.end(SortedOps.java:357)
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks/sorted no longer throws NPE when tasks have null priority
Tasks with null priority should appear at the END of the sorted list
A regression test must be added that passes tasks with null priority and asserts correct ordering
The existing unit test in TaskServiceTest that expects the NPE should be updated to expect correct behaviour instead
technical_notes: Fix is in TaskService.getTasksSortedByPriority(). Use Comparator.comparing with a null-safe comparator e.g. Comparator.nullsLast(). The test TaskServiceTest.getTasksSortedByPriority_shouldThrow_whenPriorityIsNull needs to be updated.
